description:
You are a compliance analyst who must generate a very small audit-trail proving that no **world-writable executable** files exist beneath  
/home/user/public_data.  
Create one CSV-formatted log file named  

    /home/user/audit/ww_exec_files.log  

that lists every executable file that is also world-writable.  
The log MUST follow this exact format so that automated grading can parse it:

1. It is plain text.
2. The first (and only) line is the CSV header exactly as shown (no leading/trailing spaces, no extra columns):

       path,permissions

3. For every matching file, append one new line with:
       a. The absolute path to the file.
       b. A comma.
       c. The octal permission bits shown by “stat -c %a …” (e.g. 777, 775, 755).
4. If no files match, the log MUST contain only the header line from step 2.
5. Do not add blank lines, surrounding quotes, or extra whitespace anywhere.

You have already been placed in a terminal session as the non-root user “user”.  
All necessary directories are writable by you. Produce the log with **one single terminal command** (pipelining is allowed but should be a single command line).  
When finished, you should be able to view the log with:

    cat /home/user/audit/ww_exec_files.log

and see output that matches the exact rules above.  
No other actions or files are required.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent starts):

Directories:
1. /home/user/public_data
2. /home/user/public_data/bin
3. /home/user/public_data/scripts
4. /home/user/audit                (empty, mode 755, user-writable)

Files with their permissions:
1. /home/user/public_data/clean_readme.txt      – regular file, 0644
2. /home/user/public_data/bin/tool              – regular file, 0755
3. /home/user/public_data/scripts/run.sh        – regular file, 0775
4. /home/user/public_data/scripts/unsafe.sh     – regular file, 0777  (THIS IS the only world-writable executable file)

Verification expectations after agent finishes:

File /home/user/audit/ww_exec_files.log must exist and be world-readable (0644 or more restrictive for group/others is acceptable; only owner write required).

Its exact contents must be two lines (including the newline terminating each line):

Line 1 (header):  
path,permissions⏎

Line 2:  
/home/user/public_data/scripts/unsafe.sh,777⏎

No extra characters, spaces, or lines are allowed. If the agent alters any other file or directory permissions unintentionally, grading will fail.
